_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Verify and validate that network graphics are accurate and comply with reporting policy.
reference_document_element_description: Monitor data quality within the assigned domain, document issues, initiate remediation workflows, and track resolution to closure.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-T-016 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-004 (RC, cosine: 0.448), DQ-T-013 (DQ, cosine: 0.425).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.200). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [accurate, comply, graphics, network, policy, reporting] vs WIDAI-only terms [assigned, closure, data, document, domain, initiate] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.864 (P=0.872, R=0.857). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:0.0%, N:60.5%, C:39.5%). No dominant classification for "Verify and validate that network graphics are accurate and comply with reporting" vs "Monitor data quality within the assigned domain, document issues, initiate remed" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.488 > STS 0.200 (gap +0.288). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.6%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [accurate, comply, graphics, network, policy, reporting]. WIDAI terms: [assigned, closure, data, document, domain, initiate].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.288 with low STS 0.200. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
